title: Why he’s running the Boston Marathon in memory of family treated at Dana-Farber
author: Annie Jonas
bullets: Trevor Murray, 30, from Malden, is running the 2026 Boston Marathon in memory of his father and sister-in-law.
Murray's father, Jeff, previously ran the marathon in 2018 during cancer treatment, inspiring Trevor's participation.
Both family members were treated at Dana-Farber Cancer Institute, motivating Trevor to support their work through his run.

title: MBTA announces schedule changes, detours as nearly 1 million expected to attend Sunday’s St. Patrick’s Day parade
author: Carson Lyle
bullets: MBTA Red Line will run on a rush hour schedule from 10 a.m. to 6 p.m. on parade day.
Shuttle buses will operate from South Station to South Boston, with service from 9:30 a.m. to 4:30 p.m.
Bus routes 9 and 11 will be suspended, and routes 10, 16, 17, and 47 will have detours on Sunday.

title: Boston Public Schools graduation rate reached all-time high last year, still lags behind state average
author: Molly Farrar
bullets: Boston Public Schools' graduation rate reached a record high of 81.3% in 2025, an increase from 2024's 79.7%.
Boston's graduation rate, although improved, remains 8 percentage points lower than the Massachusetts state average of 89.3%.
Improvements in graduation rates were noted across demographics, with notable increases for English learners and students with disabilities.

title: Bruins’ playoff hopes are destined to fail if they can’t solve confounding road woes
author: Conor Ryan
bullets: The Boston Bruins are struggling with an 11-14-5 road record, impacting their playoff prospects this season.
Boston's loss against the Pittsburgh Penguins highlighted their road issues, despite Pavel Zacha's hat trick performance.
The Bruins, occupying the second wild-card spot, face 12 crucial away games as they aim to secure playoff entry.

title: This Marblehead man is running the Boston Marathon for his pediatric patients
author: Carson Lyle
bullets: Brian Wishart, 42, from Marblehead, is running the 2026 Boston Marathon to support Spaulding Rehab's pediatric programs.
Wishart is Chief of Pediatric Rehabilitation at Spaulding, aiming to integrate research into clinical care for children.
Funds raised will enhance adaptive sports programs and resources for child life specialists at Spaulding Rehabilitation.

title: 8 children have now died from flu in Mass. this season
author: Carson Lyle
bullets: Eight children and 298 adults have died from the flu in Massachusetts this season, state data reveals.
Flu activity in Boston was reported as low, with moderate flu-like activity as of the last week of February.
Only 38.1% of Massachusetts residents were vaccinated against the flu by February 28, according to state health data.

title: NBA cancels Hawks’ ‘Magic City’ promotion amid backlash following Luke Kornet’s letter
author: Khari A. Thompson
bullets: NBA cancels Atlanta Hawks' 'Magic City Monday' promotion following backlash from fans and stakeholders.
Luke Kornet's viral open letter questioned the promotion's choice due to its association with an adult venue.
Event was to feature Magic City chicken wings and a performance by hip-hop artist T.I.

title: Shaquille O’Neal had no clue who Celtics guard Baylor Scheierman is
author: Khari A. Thompson
bullets: Shaquille O'Neal did not recognize Celtics guard Baylor Scheierman during an Inside the NBA broadcast.
Baylor Scheierman, a 2024 first-round pick, is averaging 4.8 points and 3.2 rebounds in 59 games this season.
Jayson Tatum acknowledged a seamless transition with the team despite being on a minutes restriction upon his return.

title: Healey calls on aviation company operating at Hanscom to cut ties with ICE
author: Ross Cristantiello
bullets: Governor Maura Healey urges Signature Aviation to end its partnership with ICE at Hanscom Field in Bedford.
Signature Aviation is identified as crucial to ICE's deportation activities at Hanscom, facilitating flights and access to the tarmac.
Healey criticizes ICE for allegedly violating rights, as Trump's administration increases deportation flights from Hanscom.

title: Delivery person falls through a shaft outside Boston’s Omni Parker House
author: Beth Treffeisen
bullets: A delivery worker fell 20 feet through a shaft outside Boston's Omni Parker House after a metal grate gave way.
Boston Fire Department rescued the conscious worker, who was then transported to a hospital for evaluation.
Boston Inspectional Services issued a building code violation and ordered structural inspections of vent grates at the hotel.
